description: Behavioural evals for task-board-forms — verifies it enriches each TASKS.md task with Done When / Status / Est fields and regenerates the ## Task Summary Table via spec-status.ts -Format task-table, idempotently (re-run replaces the auto-generated block between markers, no duplication). Grounded in the skill contract (Phase 3 step 1b).

evals.name: every-task-gets-done-when-status-est
evals.prompt: Run task-board-forms on a TASKS.md where tasks have titles + ids but no Done When / Status / Est fields.
evals.expected_output: Every task block gains a non-placeholder `Done When` (a verifiable condition, not `TBD`), a `Status` from the legal vocabulary, and an `Est`. No task is left without the three fields.

evals.name: summary-table-regenerated-between-markers
evals.prompt: Run task-board-forms on a TASKS.md that has a stale ## Task Summary Table.
evals.expected_output: The ## Task Summary Table is regenerated via spec-status.ts -Format task-table and replaces ONLY the auto-generated block between its markers; hand-written prose outside the markers is untouched.

evals.name: idempotent-rerun-no-duplicate-table-or-fields
evals.prompt: Run task-board-forms twice in a row on the same TASKS.md.
evals.expected_output: The second run produces a byte-identical result (idempotent): no duplicated Task Summary Table, no doubled Done When / Status / Est fields. The marked block is replaced, not appended.

evals.name: done-when-is-verifiable-not-restated-title
evals.prompt: Run task-board-forms on a task titled "implement the YAML writer".
evals.expected_output: The generated `Done When` is a checkable acceptance condition (e.g. "writer emits valid YAML for a 3-spec corpus and the e2e asserts it"), NOT a restatement of the title ("YAML writer implemented"). Verifies Done-When quality, not echo.

evals.name: status-values-from-legal-vocabulary
evals.prompt: Run task-board-forms and inspect the Status fields it writes.
evals.expected_output: Each Status is one of the legal lifecycle tokens (TODO / READY / IN_PROGRESS / BLOCKED / DONE) consistent with the task parser STATUS_MAP — never an ad-hoc string. A task already marked done keeps its checkbox/Status consistent.
